title: ueopen
text: ueopen Return the current Uniface environment handle that can be used to instantiate services. long ueopen (UHACT* phEnv ); Copy Parameters phEnv Uniface environment handle. This handle can be used in subsequent calls to uinstnew . After use, phEnv must be freed by calling ufreeh . (Output) Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ueopen Value Error constant Meaning 1 UINI_SUCCESS Success -1 UINI_LOAD_ERROR The Uniface environment was not initialized. -4 UINI_ALLOC_ERROR The supplied pointer is zero. We recommend that you use the mnemonic values rather than the numeric values. For example, you should test for UINI_SUCCESS rather than for 1. While this might make your code slightly harder to debug, it improves on readability and portability. Use Library: ucall Description ueopen returns the handle to the current Uniface environment, which can be used as input parameter for subsequent calls to uinstnew . Your program is responsible for freeing this handle after use. By not doing so, you could cause a memory leak, which might become an issue if you call ueopen repeatedly but do not free the handles. To free the environment handle, call ufreeh . This functions is primarily meant for C code that is called by Uniface, and for C code that needs to call in to Uniface. In this case, the environment has already been initialized by the Uniface application, so all you need to do is to get a handle. If you have created the Uniface environment yourself by calling uecreate , there is no need to call ueopen , because you can use the handle that was returned by uecreate . Although there is no point in it, it is allowed to call both uecreate and ueopen . Note that generated call-in stubs always start by calling ueopen , because they have no way of knowing whether you have explicitly called uecreate . Example The following contrived example shows how C code can call in to Uniface using the call-in API. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void TESTCALLIN (void) { UHACT hEnv; /* Uniface environment */ UHACT hIn; /* Instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handle */ long procErr; /* ProcScript error return parameter */ long opStat; /* Activate status return parameter */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get the environment handle */ uinstnew(hEnv, "MYSVC", 0, 0, /* Instantiate service MYSVC */ UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); uinstopr(hIn, "EXEC", 0, &hOp); /* Instantiate EXEC operation */ uopract (hOp, &opStat, &procErr); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel(hIn, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free the operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free the instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free the environment handle */ } Copy Related Links uecreate Initialize the Uniface runtime for component call-in. ufreeh Free (delete) a handle. uinstnew Create a new instance of a Uniface 4GL component.
